title: senecio gaspensis var. firmifolius
intro: Senecio gaspensis var. firmifolius, also known as the firm-leaved ragwort, is a rare and endangered plant species native to the Gaspe Peninsula in Quebec, Canada. The firm-leaved ragwort has been used in traditional medicine for its potential medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's leaves and flowers have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, and its extracts have been reported to have antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. Traditionally, the firm-leaved ragwort has been prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and its herbal preparations are used to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing.
